title: close Video American Accountability Foundation president releases Education Department 'watch list,' explains why Trump
raw_response: {"rating": 1.8, "lean": "Lean Right", "explanation": "This is a news report. After excluding quotations and attributed language, the author’s framing and editorial choices tilt Right, chiefly by amplifying narratives and related links favorable to conservative positions without presenting countervailing perspectives. The accumulation supports a Lean Right rating. Detailed Analysis: (1) The page leads with a video header promoting an argument for “why Trump should clean house” at the Education Department—an editorial choice that aligns with a conservative agenda; (2) The article highlights elimination of a “Biden-era” position and repeatedly notes dismissals of complaints, but offers no balancing reaction from complainants, civil rights advocates, or education groups—structural omission that favors the Right-leaning view of such removals; (3) The phrasing “Biden-era position tasked with investigating school districts and parents” frames the eliminated role as adversarial to parents, echoing a conservative narrative; (4) A “Related Article” link promotes a GOP effort to abolish the Department of Education, reinforcing a right-of-center policy frame through curated linkage; (5) Sourcing rests almost entirely on agency actions and descriptions, with no inclusion of opposing analysis or data, which results in one-sided presentation consistent with Lean Right framing."}
